name: Replay (Year-in-Review / Annual Recap UI Pattern)
overview: An annual data-driven recap UI pattern that synthesizes a user's year of activity into a personalized, story-formatted summary. Two archetypes dominate: Apple Music Replay (data-deep, static, year-round persistent dashboard, minimalist, accuracy-first) and Spotify Wrapped (festival-like, animated story cards, once-a-year cultural event, viral-share-optimized, maximalist). The pattern leverages the endowment effect, self-reflection, and social comparison to drive retention and organic acquisition.
originator_or_company: Spotify (Wrapped, launched 2015 as 'Year in Music', rebranded 2016 to Wrapped; design led by Global Brand Design Director Rebecca Lim, formerly Rasmus Wangelin). Apple (Music Replay, launched 2019 as a year-round web dashboard, expanded into in-app Highlight Reel cards in 2023). Pattern earlier popularized by Facebook 'Year in Review' (2014) and adopted by Duolingo, Strava, Netflix, YouTube Music, GitHub, Reddit Recap, etc.
key_resources: [Reinvention and evolution: Inside the design of Spotify Wrapped 2024 (It's Nice That)](https://www.itsnicethat.com/features/spotify-wrapped-2024-graphic-design-041224)
[The Art and Science Behind Spotify Wrapped (Spotify Newsroom)](https://newsroom.spotify.com/2024-12-04/the-art-and-science-behind-spotify-wrapped/)

[Spotify Wrapped campaign hit 200M engaged users in 24 hours (Music Business Worldwide)](https://www.musicbusinessworldwide.com/spotify-wrapped-campaign-hit-200m-engaged-users-in-24-hours-a-19-yoy-increase/)
[The Behavioral Science Behind Spotify Wrapped's Viral Success (The Decision Lab)](https://thedecisionlab.com/insights/consumer-insights/the-behavioral-science-behind-spotify-wrappeds-viral-success)
[Spotify Wrapped vs Apple Music Replay: What's the difference (SoundGuys)](https://www.soundguys.com/spotify-wrapped-vs-apple-music-replay-149834/)
[Spotify (Un)wrapped: how ordinary users critically reflect on Spotify's datafication (Taylor & Francis, 2024)](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/09589236.2024.2433674)
[An algorithmic event: The celebration and critique of Spotify Wrapped (SAGE, 2025)](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/14614448251391301)
[How we built a personalized Year-in-Review for millions of learners (Duolingo Engineering Blog)](https://blog.duolingo.com/year-in-review-behind-the-scenes/)

[After an AI Backlash, Spotify Wrapped Returns With a More Human Touch for 2025 (Adweek)](https://www.adweek.com/creativity/after-an-ai-backlash-spotify-wrapped-returns-with-a-more-human-touch-for-2025/)
core_mechanics: The Replay pattern has two dominant implementations with opposite design DNA. (1) Spotify Wrapped (festive/dynamic): vertical full-screen story cards modeled on Instagram/Snapchat Stories, auto-advancing 3-7 seconds each, 15-25 cards per session. Top artists, top songs, top genres, minutes listened, top % of fans for an artist, Listening Personality (introduced 2022, scaled back 2025), Music Evolution (2024, tracks listening shifts across phases of the year), live multiplayer compare-with-friends (2024). Heavy use of bespoke typeface (Spotify Mix, 2024), saturated gradients, kinetic typography, looping shapes. Each card has a 'Share this story' button hard-wired to TikTok / Instagram / X with auto-formatted vertical assets. Released first week of December as a single global drop event. (2) Apple Music Replay (analytic/persistent): year-round web dashboard at replay.music.apple.com plus in-app Highlight Reel. Updates weekly Jan-Dec. Annual Highlight Reel released in early December with animated cards (top artists, songs, albums, genres) plus 2024-added Listening Streak (longest continuous listening period), First Listened Date (first time you played an artist/song), Monthly Replays (top per month), Top Artist Streak (consecutive months), Top 100/500/1000 fan ranking. Background music plays during the reel relevant to each stat. Subdued gradients, system-style typography, share cards exist but are utilitarian. Both patterns end with a generated personalized playlist persisting in the user's library. Underlying tech: pre-computed Spark/BigQuery jobs aggregating 365 days of event logs, served as static JSON to the client to avoid load spikes; rendering happens client-side with native animation libraries (Lottie / Reanimated equivalents).
designer_or_researcher_voice: Rebecca Lim (Spotify Global Brand Design Director, 2024): 'The major inspiration for Wrapped is always the fans, artists, creators and authors that make this moment the phenomenon that it is... The 2024 Wrapped design is the result of an incredible collaborative effort.' She describes the 2024 direction as 'a dynamic, high-energy color palette that celebrates the year's momentum, with layers blending and shifting to create something entirely new that scales exponentially.' On the brief: every year the in-house team is 'challenged to create a design system and art direction for not just a global marketing campaign but for what's today a massive cultural moment, needing to be visually and conceptually unique and unexpected while being a hard-working system shipped to 170 countries.' Spotify Newsroom (2024): 'No one wants to unwrap the same thing every year' — the team intentionally reinvents the visual language annually. Behavioral researcher (The Decision Lab): 'Wrapped amplifies the endowment effect by reflecting your own data back to you as a shareable story... users are more likely to stick with Spotify, feeling that their account has unique value that can't be easily replicated elsewhere.' Critical academic voice (Annabell & Vindum Rasmussen, SAGE 2025): users describe Listening Personality as 'reductive and shallow due to their genericity,' drawing 'connections to other types of classification they were critical of, including MBTI, star signs, tarot cards and Buzzfeed quizzes.' [uncertain] Apple has not published designer interviews about Replay; the design philosophy is inferred from product behavior and press coverage describing it as 'minimalist, elegant, accuracy-first'.
metrics_or_evidence: Spotify Wrapped 2024: 200M engaged users within ~62 hours (19% YoY increase per Music Business Worldwide); ~500M shares (+41% YoY); ~2.1M social mentions in 48h; 400M+ TikTok views in 3 days. 2023 reached 227M engaged monthly active users. 2025 hit 250M engagements in under 3 days (Variety). Spotify reports Wrapped is the single largest annual driver of new app installs and an inflection point for MAU. Duolingo Year in Review: company reports millions of learners receive it; specific share counts not public. [uncertain] Apple Music Replay engagement metrics are not publicly disclosed by Apple. General benchmarks: app users who receive a personalized notification within the first 90 days are 3x more likely to stick around (Adapty 2026). No published RCT isolates the causal retention lift of a year-in-review feature; evidence is observational + company case study.
criticisms: (1) Novelty effect: the spike is concentrated in a single week each year; engagement returns to baseline within ~10 days. (2) Surveillance normalization: Wrapped trains users to feel comfortable being measured ('innocuous surveillance' — NYU Comm Club, 2023). (3) Reductive personality categorization: Listening Personality criticized as MBTI/Buzzfeed-quiz-tier oversimplification (Annabell & Vindum Rasmussen, 2025). (4) Performance over enjoyment: critics argue Wrapped promotes 'broadcasting taste' rather than music enjoyment. (5) AI hallucination risk: 2024 AI-generated Wrapped (NotebookLM podcast) produced absurd numbers and removed beloved features (top genres), causing widespread backlash and forcing 2025 reversal. (6) Quantitative fixation: rewarding users for raw minutes can encourage compulsive use over meaningful use. (7) Algorithmic opacity: users see results but not how stats are computed, so errors (mis-attributed top artists) erode trust. (8) Wrapped is not a randomized intervention — published causal evidence is thin.
failure_cases: Spotify Wrapped 2024 widely panned: heavy AI generation produced hallucinated stats and an AI podcast that misread numbers ('five hundred and four hundred and ninety eighty-one minutes'); top genres were removed and replaced with vague AI 'Listening Characters'; Spotify publicly reversed course in 2025 (Adweek, Fortune). Spotify Wrapped 2025 still suffered glitches and 'AI slop with paid stats' complaints (Headphonesty, Inc.). Facebook 'Year in Review' 2014 famously surfaced photos of dead family members and traumatic events to users in cheerful frames; Eric Meyer's 'Inadvertent Algorithmic Cruelty' essay forced industry-wide reckoning on positive-only assumptions. Reddit Recap consistently underperforms vs Spotify because reddit content is text-heavy and harder to abstract into shareable visuals. Apple Music Replay has been criticized as visually 'too safe' and 'plain', failing to generate viral moments despite better data accuracy.
antipatterns: (1) Positive-only framing without sensitivity guards (Facebook 2014 lesson): assume some users had a hard year; allow opt-out; never auto-pop modal on app open. (2) Hallucinated/AI-generated stats without verification (Spotify 2024 lesson): if AI is used, ground every number in deterministic queries. (3) Forced sharing dark patterns: full-screen 'share to continue' walls. (4) Comparison shaming: 'You studied 30% less than your friends' framing — reverses motivation, especially for women in their 20s. (5) Streak-only narratives: glorifying perfect streaks shames anyone who broke one (regulatory and ethical risk in education). (6) Fabricated personality buckets (MBTI-style 'You are a Visionary Learner'): users see through it as Buzzfeed-tier and trust drops. (7) Surprise notifications timed to coincide with traumatic anniversaries. (8) Locking the recap behind paywall during the share moment — kills the viral loop and feels exploitative. (9) Means-ends reversal: optimizing minutes-of-app-use as the headline metric instead of learning outcome metrics.
applicability_to_shikaq: Highly applicable, but the Apple Music Replay archetype is the correct reference for shikaq, not Spotify Wrapped. Translate as 'shikaq Year in Review' / '今年の学びサマリー' (also viable as quarter-in-review or course-completion-recap). Implementation: a Today-tab entry-point card in late December showing animated SwiftUI/Reanimated cards with: (a) total questions answered, (b) minutes studied, (c) top subject/section, (d) longest streak, (e) accuracy curve over the year, (f) toughest concept conquered, (g) first-touched date for current target qualification, (h) percentile vs same-qualification cohort (anonymous), ending with a personalized 'Next year's plan' suggestion and an exportable share card. HEXAD mapping: serves Achiever (mastery proof), Philanthropist (share to encourage friends studying same qualification), Free Spirit (self-reflection), Player (percentile rank). Critical adaptations for the 20代女性 / iPad / Apple HIG persona: use Apple Music Replay's restraint (Liquid Glass cards, SF Pro typography, semantic colors, Spring animation, tasteful score) rather than Spotify Wrapped's TikTok-bait maximalism — the persona is 'スタバで参考書を広げる', not '17歳の TikTok ユーザー'. Avoid personality buckets ('あなたは情熱型学習者'). Avoid leaderboard shaming. Allow generation of an anonymized share card optimized for X/Threads/Instagram in a calm pastel palette. Keep a year-round persistent stats hub (Apple Music Replay style) at /summary, then layer the December annual reel on top. Ethical guard: hide the recap if the user studied < 5 days that year (avoid shaming). For 簿記2級 / 基本情報 long-prep cycles, also offer 'Course Completion Replay' triggered when a course finishes, not just calendar-year.
phase_assignment: Phase 3 (6-8週). Reasoning: requires substantial accumulated data (full year or full course), aggregation pipeline, animation work, and share-card asset templates. Useless until shikaq has retained users for at least one full study cycle, so it should follow Phase 1 (core learning loop) and Phase 2 (streaks/practice gamification). However a lightweight 'Course Completion Replay' (3-5 cards on completing a course) could ship in late Phase 2 as a precursor.
integration_points: Components: new <YearInReviewModal /> launched from <TodayContent /> hero card (Dec 1 - Jan 15 window) and from <Sidebar /> Summary entry; reuses <ActivityRings /> for the rings card, <GoalRing /> for goal completion card, <MaterialIcon /> for icon set. New <ReplayCard /> story-card primitive with Spring transitions via react-native-reanimated. New screen route /replay/[year] in Expo Router. DB tables touched (read-only for the recap): quiz_results (count, accuracy curve, hardest question), progress (lessons completed, course completion dates), profiles (display_name, target certifications), bookmarks (curated favorites). New table: replay_snapshots (user_id, year, payload jsonb, generated_at) — pre-computed nightly via Supabase Edge Function or Postgres scheduled function (pg_cron) to avoid query spikes during Dec launch. New Edge Function: generate-replay (called nightly between Nov 15 - Dec 31 for users with sufficient data). Share card export: react-native-view-shot + native share sheet on iOS, generates PNG from a hidden offscreen <ShareCard /> component. RLS: user can only read their own snapshot, admin role can read all. Reuses existing tintColor mapping (itPassport / fe / spi / boki) for category-colored cards.
risks: (1) Implementation difficulty: animation polish to reach 'Apple-grade' bar is high; Spring tuning, type animation, and Liquid Glass background work consume significant engineering. (2) Data sparsity: a user who joined in November has no meaningful year — must gate by minimum activity threshold or risk an embarrassing recap. (3) Scope creep: Replay easily expands into AI-generated narration, share-card variants, multiplayer compare — keep ruthlessly minimal. (4) Ethical: shaming users who didn't study much; mitigate with empathetic copy and opt-out. (5) Persona mismatch: if visuals drift toward Spotify Wrapped maximalism (TikTok-bait gradients, kinetic type), it conflicts with Apple-HIG-only design rule and the 20代女性 / カフェ / iPad persona. (6) Pre-compute cost: aggregating 365 days of quiz_results across all users in a single nightly window can spike DB load; needs incremental snapshots. (7) Memory/perf on older iPad: 20+ cards with Liquid Glass and Reanimated can drop frames on iPad 8th gen — must benchmark and provide reduced-motion fallback. (8) Privacy: percentile-vs-cohort comparisons must be k-anonymized (don't compute against cohorts of <50 users).
apple_hig_compatibility: compatible (when implemented in the Apple Music Replay archetype). Rationale: Apple Music Replay is itself a first-party Apple product following HIG, so adopting its restraint, year-round persistence, Liquid Glass cards, SF Pro typography, semantic colors, Spring animation, and tasteful background music is a direct fit. Spotify Wrapped's maximalist archetype (saturated gradients, custom display typeface, kinetic typography, autoplay vertical Stories) conflicts with HIG's Clarity/Deference/Depth foundations and must be avoided. The annual Highlight Reel is best implemented as a sheet/modal rather than full-screen takeover (HIG-aligned), with a swipe-to-dismiss affordance.
ethical_score: neutral. Positive: encourages self-reflection, provides genuine sense of accomplishment, surfaces progress that would otherwise be invisible, can motivate continued study. Negative: novelty-driven engagement spike (not durable retention); risks shaming low-activity users; can normalize quantified-self surveillance; share-pressure can feel performative; comparison features can trigger anxiety in achievement-oriented 20代女性 segment. Burnout risk is low for an annual event but elevated for course-completion replays if they push immediate next-course enrollment. Time-regret: users generally report Wrapped as a positive moment, not a regret-inducing one. Mitigations: opt-out toggle, no leaderboard shaming, empathetic copy for low-activity users, no auto-popup during sensitive periods (e.g. exam result release dates).
evidence_quality: company_case_study (primary). Spotify and Duolingo publish engagement numbers but not isolated causal effects. Two recent academic papers (Annabell & Vindum Rasmussen 2025 SAGE; Spotify (Un)wrapped 2024 Taylor & Francis) provide qualitative critical analysis. No rigorous_RCT directly tests year-in-review feature retention lift. Evidence on underlying mechanisms (endowment effect, social comparison, self-reflection) is well-established in behavioral economics literature.
localization_fit_japan: high. Japanese 20代女性 are heavy users of self-reflective annual rituals (年末の振り返り, 手帳文化, 一年の総括), making Year-in-Review a culturally resonant pattern. Both Spotify Wrapped and Apple Music Replay are widely shared in Japanese X/Instagram each December — the format is already familiar. Apple Music Replay's restrained aesthetic aligns with Japanese preference for わびさび-adjacent minimalism (vs. North American maximalism). However, sharing behavior is more private in Japan: many users prefer screenshot-then-private-DM over public viral posts; share-card design should optimize for 1:1 LINE/private Instagram Story sharing rather than public X virality. Avoid overt percentile rankings (Japanese users are sensitive to public comparison); soft framing ('上位30%の頑張り') tested better than hard rank ('312位'). Hiragino Sans / SF Pro Japanese fallback already required by shikaq — fits naturally.
